metadata.name: Porcelain
metadata.title: Porcelain Laser Cleaning Settings
metadata.description: Detailed machine settings, parameter relationships, diagnostic procedures, and troubleshooting guides for optimizing porcelain laser cleaning operations.

author.name: Ikmanda Roswati
author.title: Ph.D.
author.jobTitle: Junior Research Scientist in Laser Physics
expertise: Ultrafast Laser Physics and Material Interactions
credentials: 2+ years in ultrafast laser research including ASEAN optics workshops
author.imageAlt: Ikmanda Roswati, Ph.D., Junior Research Scientist in Laser Physics at Bandung Institute of Technology, fieldwork optics setup

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Porcelain surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for Porcelain absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on Porcelain surfaces

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage and thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal while preserving Porcelain substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with controlled thermal effects

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning efficiency

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning coverage without excessive thermal buildup

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning with thermal control
